decks.notes: The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent through a combination of low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, and combat tricks like Mutagenic Growth and Violent Urge. The deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain a fast pace throughout the game, using cards like Slickshot Show-Off to further enhance its aggressive strategy. The deck also includes card selection and manipulation spells like Expressive Iteration and Preordain to ensure consistency and efficiency in drawing the necessary cards to execute its game plan.

The main goal of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures, such as Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones, using cards like Faithless Looting and Thought Scour. Once these creatures are in the graveyard, the deck utilizes cards like Persist and Unearth to bring them back onto the battlefield with an advantage. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Spell Pierce to help protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck focuses on reanimating key creatures quickly and efficiently to overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory.

The focus of this deck is to disrupt your opponent's plans and control the game through counterspells, removal, and hand disruption. By utilizing a mix of control elements such as counterspells like Counterspell and Force of Negation, removal spells like Fatal Push and Quantum Riddler, and hand disruption like Thoughtseize, the deck aims to disrupt your opponent's strategy and maintain control of the game. With threats like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares, the deck also has the capability to close out the game once it has gained control.

The deck focuses on maximizing the aggro potential of Eldrazi creatures, using cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Sire of Seven Deaths to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It also includes disruption elements like Kozilek's Command and Chalice of the Void to control the game and protect the Eldrazi creatures. With a mana base that supports the Eldrazi theme and provides consistent access to colored mana, this deck aims to consistently apply pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan in order to secure a quick victory.

The key concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly establish board presence with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, while using support cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to control the battlefield. The deck also utilizes Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer for additional card advantage and utility. With a mix of direct damage spells and powerful enchantments like Ocelot Pride and Arena of Glory, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient creatures and strategic plays to secure victory.

This deck focuses on using the Twin combo with Deceiver Exarch and Splinter Twin to create an infinite number of hasty tokens. It also includes various control elements like Counterspell and Lightning Bolt to disrupt opponents' strategies. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer provide card advantage and extra value. The deck is designed to quickly assemble the combo while keeping the opponent's threats in check, allowing for explosive and decisive wins. It aims to control the game until it can safely execute its winning combo.

The deck focuses on control through counterspells and removal, with cards like Counterspell, Force of Negation, and Lightning Bolt to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian provide card advantage, while Galvanic Discharge and Invert Polarity offer additional removal and board control. The deck also includes cards like Blood Moon and Otawara, Soaring City to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck aims to control the game and outvalue the opponent through efficient removal and card advantage.

This deck focuses on utilizing UrzaTron lands to quickly ramp up mana and cast powerful Eldrazi creatures and colorless spells. The goal is to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Chalice of the Void, while also putting pressure on them with Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Spirit Dragon. With a mix of powerful threats and disruptive elements, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through a combination of control and aggression.

The deck focuses on quickly generating mana through spells like Pyretic Ritual and Desperate Ritual, using cards like Ruby Medallion to accelerate the combo. It also utilizes cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Grapeshot to deal damage to the opponent. Past in Flames allows for recursion of spells from the graveyard, while Wrenn's Resolve and Artist's Talent provide additional utility and card draw. The deck aims to assemble the combo pieces quickly to create a powerful storm turn and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and combo pieces to generate mana, draw cards, and assemble a win condition. It utilizes cards like Urza, Lord High Artificer, Emry, Lurker of the Loch, and Mishra's Bauble to generate mana and draw cards. The combo pieces include Grinding Station and Urza's Saga to assemble a win condition. Cards like Metallic Rebuke provide interaction and disruption, while cards like Swan Song and Soul-Guide Lantern provide protection and graveyard hate. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan to win the game efficiently.

This UR Control deck focuses on maintaining board control through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan by countering key spells and removing threats with cards like Unholy Heat and Lightning Bolt. By controlling the game and managing resources effectively, the deck seeks to outvalue the opponent and secure victory through incremental advantage. Additionally, the deck utilizes cards like Mishra's Bauble and Snapcaster Mage to generate additional value and maintain card advantage throughout the game.

This deck focuses on quickly putting pressure on the opponent with aggressive creatures like Phelia and Ragavan while also disrupting their game plan with interactive spells like Prismatic Ending and Teferi, Time Raveler. It aims to control the pace of the game by utilizing cards like Solitude and Consign to Memory to handle threats while maintaining a board presence. It also has a slight element of combo potential with Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Meticulous Archive. The deck is designed to be efficient and versatile, adapting to different matchup scenarios to come out on top.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts and utilizing them for value while pressuring the opponent. It uses cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Goblin Engineer to recur artifacts from the graveyard and has synergy with cards like Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble. Urza's Saga provides additional artifact generation and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student helps with card selection and filtering. The deck looks to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of early aggression and powerful artifact-based interactions.

This deck utilizes powerful one-drop creatures like Nethergoyf and Death's Shadow to apply early pressure on the opponent while disrupting their hand with cards like Thoughtseize and Sheoldred's Edict. The deck also features a low land count and efficient removal spells like Fatal Push to keep the opponent on the back foot. Overall, the deck aims to control the game by applying constant pressure and disrupting the opponent's game plan, ultimately overwhelming them with efficient threats and disruption.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with Birthing Ritual and utilizing Simic creatures with powerful abilities to overwhelm the opponent. Cards like Misty Rainforest and Verdant Catacombs help thin the deck for consistency, while Shardless Agent and Coiling Oracle provide card advantage. Force of Negation and Flare of Denial help control the board, while Ice-Fang Coatl and Harbinger of the Seas provide additional versatility. The deck aims to quickly establish a strong board presence and control the game with a combination of aggro and disruption.

The Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive play, utilizing creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to deal quick damage to opponents. It also makes use of burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to control the board and finish off opponents. The deck's mana base includes fetch lands like Arid Mesa and Marsh Flats to ensure consistent mana fixing. Additionally, cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provide additional value and support for the aggressive game plan. With a mix of creatures and spells, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and relentless assault.
